3.why: We clone each of them exactly once.

4.happening: Preparing the node list for the drawing.
4.why: Each node will be drawn as a labelled circle.

5.happening: Walking through every node to draw it.
5.why: Each node becomes a labelled circle.

6.why: Each node's label is its 1-based value.

7.happening: Preparing the edge list for the drawing.

8.happening: Scanning each node's neighbours to build edges.
8.why: Every adjacency becomes a drawn edge.

9.why: These are the nodes it connects to.

10.happening: Looking at each neighbour in turn.
10.why: Each adjacency may become an edge.

11.why: It is one endpoint of a potential edge.

12.happening: Edge {i + 1}-{nb} is new (smaller endpoint first) — draw it.
12.why: Adding each undirected edge once avoids duplicates.

12.happening: Edge {i + 1}-{nb} was already added from the other side — skip it.
12.why: The reverse direction was recorded earlier.

13.why: The drawing now shows this connection.

14.why: The clone will mirror the original's connections.
14.invariant: result will hold each clone's neighbours.

15.happening: Making an empty neighbour slot for every clone.
15.why: Each clone starts with no edges.

16.happening: Adding an empty neighbour list for a clone.
16.why: Its edges are filled during the BFS.

17.why: It records which nodes already have a clone.
17.invariant: cloned pairs each original with its copy.

18.happening: The graph is empty — nothing to clone.
18.why: An empty graph clones to an empty graph.

18.happening: The graph has nodes — begin the breadth-first clone.
18.why: BFS visits every node starting from node 1.
18.invariant: Every reachable node will be cloned.

19.why: There were no nodes to copy.

20.happening: Starting the BFS queue with node 1.
20.why: Node 1 is the entry point of the graph.
20.invariant: The queue holds nodes waiting to be processed.

21.happening: head points at the front of the queue.
21.why: Processing advances head instead of removing items.
21.invariant: Nodes before head are already processed.

22.happening: Cloning node 1 and recording the pairing.
22.why: The clone has the same label but is a brand-new node.

23.happening: Processing the queue until every node is handled.
23.why: BFS explores the graph outward from node 1.
23.invariant: The queue drains as nodes are processed.

24.happening: Taking node {queue[head]} from the front of the queue.
24.why: This node's edges will be copied next.
24.invariant: node is the one being processed.

25.happening: Advancing the queue front past it.
25.why: This node is now being processed.

26.why: Each neighbour's edge is copied to the clone.

27.happening: Copying each neighbour edge in turn.
27.why: The clone gets the same connections.

28.why: Its edge into the clone is copied next.

29.why: The clone mirrors the original's edges.
29.invariant: result for this node gains a neighbour.

30.why: Each node must be cloned exactly once.
30.invariant: already records whether a clone exists.

31.happening: Node {nb} is new — clone it and queue it.
31.why: First sight of a node triggers its clone.
31.invariant: Every node is cloned exactly once.

31.happening: Node {nb} already has a clone — just reuse the pairing.
31.why: A cycle led back to an already-cloned node.

32.happening: Cloning node {nb} and recording the pairing.
32.why: A brand-new node is created for the clone.

33.happening: Queueing node {nb} to copy its edges later.
33.why: BFS will visit it and copy its edges.

34.why: Every node and edge has been copied.
34.invariant: The clone is a deep copy of the original.

init.why: We need the node count before cloning.

loop.happening: Iterating over nodes, neighbours, or the BFS queue.
loop.why: Each pass builds part of the clone.
loop.invariant: Every node and edge is handled once.

check.happening: Testing an edge, emptiness, or whether a node is already cloned.
check.why: These decisions avoid duplicates and handle empty graphs.
check.invariant: Each node is cloned exactly once.

move.why: The processed node is left behind.

update.happening: Building nodes, edges, clones, or copying an edge.
update.why: The clone and its map grow step by step.
update.invariant: cloned mirrors which originals are copied.

return.why: Every reachable node has been copied.
return.invariant: The clone is a deep copy.
